title: Scaphyglottis crurigera
taxonomy: Scaphyglottis crurigera is a species of orchid found in Central and South America. This orchid belongs to the family Orchidaceae. The genus Scaphyglottis is a part of the subfamily Epidendroideae. Within the orchid family, Scaphyglottis crurigera is classified as a terrestrial orchid, meaning it grows on the ground. Its classification also includes being a type of epiphytic orchid, although it is more commonly found growing in terrestrial environments.

intro: Scaphyglottis crurigera, commonly known as the "strap orchid," is a species of flowering plant in the orchid family (Orchidaceae). The strap orchid has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its purported health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various health conditions such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Scaphyglottis crurigera include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal preparations, the strap orchid is often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to take advantage of its potential health benefits.
